steps.title: Set up the conditional probability equation
steps.content:
When choosing a rhesus negative person, we need the probability of type B: $$P(\text{B} \mid \text{rhesus } -) = \frac{\text{people with B and rhesus } -}{\text{total rhesus negative}}$$ From the table: $P(\text{B} \mid \text{rhesus } -) = \frac{2}{7 + 2 + 1 + x} = \frac{2}{10 + x}$.

We're told this equals $\frac{1}{9}$: $$\frac{2}{10 + x} = \frac{1}{9}$$
steps.formula: $$P(A \mid B) = \frac{\text{count of both A and B}}{\text{total count of B}}$$
